description:
You are a Linux systems-engineer who has just finished a round of network-stack hardening.  
    As a quick sanity-check you want to make sure that the latency to the local TCP/IP stack
    has not been negatively impacted.  
    Perform a **single, one-liner terminal command** (it may use pipes and redirections
    but must be executed as one line) that:

    1. Sends exactly five ICMP echo requests to the local host ( 127.0.0.1 ).  
    2. Extracts only the *average* round-trip time (RTT) value reported by the
       `ping` summary line (the line that contains “min/avg/max/mdev”).  
    3. Writes that value to the file

           /home/user/benchmarks/localhost_ping.bench

       – creating the parent directory “/home/user/benchmarks” if it does not already exist.  
    4. The file **must contain exactly one line, formatted precisely like this**:

           avg_rtt_ms:<number_with_at_least_one_decimal_digit>

       Examples of valid content (do **not** hard-code these numbers; they are just examples):

           avg_rtt_ms:0.041
           avg_rtt_ms:0.23
           avg_rtt_ms:1.004

       • No spaces may appear before or after the colon.  
       • A trailing newline character **is required**.  
       • Nothing else should be written to the file.

    The goal is simply to capture the post-hardening average RTT so it can be
    archived for later comparison.

    When you are done, `cat /home/user/benchmarks/localhost_ping.bench`
    should print exactly one line that follows the format above.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

    1. Directory:  /home/user/benchmarks
       • Must exist and be accessible to the regular user.

    2. File:       /home/user/benchmarks/localhost_ping.bench
       • Must exist.
       • File content must match the regular expression  
         ^avg_rtt_ms:[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?\n$
       • No additional lines or characters are permitted.

    The test harness will:
       a. check that the directory exists,
       b. check that the file exists,
       c. validate that the file’s single line matches the regex above.
